plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure as per conservative mandate. Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure. Buy XLK at 10% to add a high-quality tech index with a strong scanner setup.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and reduced volatility. It significantly lowers leverage and concentration, aiming for steady, low-drawdown performance. Expect smaller gains but also smaller potential losses, with a focus on protecting principal.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage. Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged and in a downtrend. Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming, rebalancing the portfolio. Keep SPAL at 5% to maintain a small leveraged ETF exposure within limits.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still allowing for strategic growth in quality tech. Expect moderate returns with controlled risk, balancing upside potential with drawdown management.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits, freeing capital for higher growth. Increase SOXL to 10% and SPAL to 5% to leverage high-beta sectors, strictly adhering to stops. Add to CRWD to 15% to press a working position with strong P&L.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, focusing on stretched growth with higher risk. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring tech setups. Be aware that this approach amplifies both potential gains and losses, with a higher probability of significant drawdown and daily decay in leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure. Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure. Maximize QQQI to 35% for increased income and trim QQQ to 10% to reallocate to income-focused holdings. This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes steady monthly distributions from income ETFs and broad index exposure, with minimal exposure to leverage or high-beta single names. While aiming for lower drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns. This is for income, not a savings account.
